rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER DAMONTE VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, MARCH 5, 2008 LINE 10. DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND HEATHER FONG'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED AS TO SECOND AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION WHICH WERE ADDED CONTRARY TO THE ORDER OF THE COURT; ABSENT THE FILING AND GRANTING OF A REQUEST FOR LEAVE TO AMEND, PLAINTIFF MAY NOT AMEND THESE CAUSES OF ACTION. SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE THIRD AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION. AS PLEAD (IN PARTICULAR PARAGRAPHS SIX AND NINE THROUGH TWELVE) ALL OF CHIEF FONG'S ACTIONS WERE IN CONNECTION WITH THE DISCIPLINARY PROCESS AND ARE SUBJECT TO A QUALIFIED IMMUNITY. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PLEAD A COHERENT SET OF FACTS THAT ALLEGE A CLAIM NOT SUBJECT TO IMMUNITY. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, MARCH 5, 2008 LINE 12. DEFENDANT JOHN VAREL'S MOTION To Quash Service Of Summons: Varel's motion to quash is moot for Varel acknowledges that now he has been personally served. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: PC SPECIALISTS, INC., DBA TECHNOLOGY INTEGRATION VS. FUSIONSTORM, A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn & Mtn To Compel Arbitration & Stay Court Proceedings, Petition,
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, MARCH 5, 2008 LINE 12. DEFENDANT MICHAEL DRAGONI'S MOTION To Compel Arbitration & Stay Court Proceedings: Motions to compel arbitration by Dragoni, Talley, Petroski, King, Eife, Barber: Denied. The First Amended Complaint names parties who are not parties to the arbitration agreement and alleges a common course of conduct that has as its central allegation the misappropriation of plaintiff's trade secrets and acts of unfair competition. The employment agreement at paragraph 7 excludes injunctive and equitable relief for such conduct from the agreement to arbitrate. Pursuant to CCP section 1281.2(c), the Court finds that conflicting rulings may well result if the requested arbitration proceeds. All of the relevant parties are involved in the litigation, both those subject to the arbitration agreement and those who are not. The pending litigation provides the optimal forum to resolve this dispute. Plaintiff's request for injunctive relief would be significantly impacted if this action was stayed pending arbitration. For these reasons, the Court denies the motion and stays the request to arbitrate pending the outcome of the court action. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: PC SPECIALISTS, INC., DBA TECHNOLOGY INTEGRATION VS. FUSIONSTORM, A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Quash Service Of Summons
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, MARCH 5, 2008 LINE 2. DEFENDANT JOHN TALLEY MOTION To Quash Service Of Summons: Motions to quash by Talley, Barber, King and Petroski: Denied. A general appearance was made on behalf of the "defendants" when counsel submitted two case management statements. Mansour v. Superior Court (1995) 38 Cal.App.4th 1750, 1756. Consistent with this appearance is counsel's representation to the Court at a law and motion hearing that counsel represented the other "defendants". Further, the employment contract calls for any arbitration to occur in California. These defendants have sought to enforce that order and by doing so, have made it clear that they are prepared to submit to the jurisdiction of this Court for any arbitration award would be enforced by this Court. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: VENTURE STRATEGY MANAGEMENT COMPANY II LLC, A et al VS. JOANNA REES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, MARCH 5, 2008 LINE 14. DEFENDANTS JOANNA REES, JOHN HAMM AND, VENTURE STRATEGY GROUP, L.L.C.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. IN GROSSET V WENAAS 2008 WL 383196, THE CALIFORNIA SUPREME COURT HOLDS THE PLAINTIFF MUST BE A CONTINUOUS OWNER TO MAINTAIN A DERIVATIVE ACTION. THE EXCEPTIONS TO THE RULE - A FRAUDULENT MERGER - IS NOT APPLICABLE TO PLAINTIFF, WHO ALLEGES THAT HE WAS FORCED TO RESIGN DUE TO PRESSURE FROM CERTAIN DEFENDANTS. THE COMPLAINT ALSO FAILS TO ALLEGE FACTS SUPPORTING THE ALTER EGO CLAIM AS TO V.S.G. =(302/PJM/AY)
